# REGIME READ — 2026-07-20

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 4 → 4 (unchanged) — but the internals deteriorated further, not stabilized.** The prior read's Stage-3 re-stabilization path (25% probability) required VXN back through 25 AND VIX sub-16 AND a TQQQ 200DMA reclaim. **VXN went the other way — 27.34 → 29.03 (+6.18%), a fresh high for this episode.** The bullish path is further away, not closer.
- **THE DOMINANT FACT: VXN made a new episode high while VIX FELL — the two vol gauges diverged sharply, and the divergence is Nasdaq-specific.** VXN 29.03 (+6.18%) vs VIX 17.84 (−4.95%). **This is the single most informative datum in the snapshot:** broad-market fear *eased* while Nasdaq-specific fear *accelerated*. The prior read's governing tension — "concentrated AI/semis derating vs no broad-market capitulation" — has now resolved decisively toward **contained-but-intensifying**. The stress is not spreading; it is *deepening where it already was*.
- **The Nasdaq drawdown extended and widened its gap to the rest of the tape.** NDX −7.1% vs 52W high (from −5.6%), Nasdaq Composite −6.1% (from −4.8%) — **both extended ~1.5pp further** — while **SPX held −2.1% and DJI −2.1%**. The dispersion between NDX (−7.1%) and SPX/DJI (−2.1%) is now **5 percentage points**, the widest of this sequence.
- **All five indices red again, NDX worst again — a second consecutive all-red, Nasdaq-led session.** SPX −1.01%, Comp −1.40%, NDX −1.49%, DJI −0.77%, Russell −0.42%. Note the broadening: DJI's loss deepened from −0.20% to −0.77%, Russell from −0.06% to −0.42%. The damage is still tech-concentrated but is no longer *exclusively* tech.
- **Total score: −4 → −4 (unchanged), but the composition shifted.** Dealer deteriorates further (−1 → −2) on the VXN new high; Cross-asset improves (−1 → 0) as **Brent FELL to 86.98 (−1.27%)**, failing to hold the re-ignition and undercutting the oil-hedge thesis for the second time in three reads. The two offset. **A flat total masking real internal movement — flagged rather than smoothed over.**
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — VXN re-closes below 25:** **FAILED, decisively.** VXN rose to 29.03, a new episode high.
  - **Bullish — VIX reclaims sub-16:** **NOT FIRED, but moved toward it.** VIX 17.84 (−4.95%) — falling, still above 16.
  - **Bullish — TQQQ reclaims 200DMA/$68:** **UNRESOLVED — no live TQQQ price available this read** (see data-integrity note). Cannot be confirmed either way.
  - **Bullish — semis stabilize (NDX green day):** **FAILED.** NDX −1.49%, worst index again.
  - **Bearish — VIX clears 20 and holds:** **NOT FIRED — moved away.** VIX fell to 17.84.
  - **Bearish — VXN toward 30:** **ESSENTIALLY FIRED.** 29.03 is ~1 point from 30, up +6.18%.
  - **Bearish — TQQQ presses 13-wk low (~$57):** **UNRESOLVED — no live price.**
  - **Bearish — Brent closes above 86 with named Gulf escalation:** **CLEARED/FAILED.** Brent 86.98 but **−1.27%, falling**, and no named escalation retrieved. The mechanical 86 level is exceeded but the *impulse* reversed — scored as failed, not fired.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL UNMEASURED. Week 12.**
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer −1 → **−2** (VXN new episode high at 29.03, TQQQ IV est. ~87%); Cross-asset −1 → **0** (Brent −1.27% failed the re-ignition; gold +0.48% only marginally supportive). Macro held −1, Breadth held −1, Sentiment/Positioning/Geopolitical held 0.

> **Snapshot mix (~1-session spread):** equities + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 17 close**; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 20 intraday.** **This read the spread cuts BOTH ways and must be handled carefully:** the bearish evidence (VXN 29.03, NDX −7.1%) is the *older* Jul-17 close, while the *fresher* Jul-20 gauges (VIX −4.95%, Brent −1.27%) are mildly constructive. **The VIX/VXN divergence is therefore partly a timing artifact** — VIX is a session fresher than VXN. This is an honest limitation, not a suppressed one: a Jul-20 VXN print could be materially lower. The read below is written so that the Nasdaq-specific conclusion does not depend solely on that comparison — NDX's −7.1%-vs-SPX's−2.1% dispersion is measured on the *same* Jul-17 close and is independent of the timing gap.
>
> **Headline #1 — the divergence is the story, with the caveat above.** VXN at a new episode high while VIX falls says the market is pricing Nasdaq risk, not systemic risk. Even discounting the one-session offset, the *level* gap (VXN 29 vs VIX 18) and the *drawdown* gap (NDX −7.1% vs SPX −2.1%) both confirm concentration.
>
> **Headline #2 — the AI/semis derating deepened rather than resolved.** NDX and Comp each extended ~1.5pp further from their highs in one session. Two consecutive all-red, Nasdaq-led sessions. No stabilization signature.
>
> **Headline #3 — the oil-hedge thesis failed again.** Brent's Jul-17 "+2.01% fourth impulse" reversed to −1.27%. This is the second time in three reads the oil re-ignition has been called and then faded. **Cross-asset is upgraded back to 0 accordingly — the prior read's −1 on that basis is retracted.**
>
> **Headline #4 — one genuine constructive: VIX fell nearly 5%.** Broad-market vol is easing. This is the only real bullish datum in the snapshot and it is given its due — it is why Stage 5 remains firmly withheld.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (risk-off, Nasdaq-concentrated).** Unchanged from prior read. **Stage 5 is withheld more confidently than last read, not less:** Stage 5 requires "VIX>20 sustained + credit widening," and **VIX moved *away* from 20 (17.84, −4.95%)**. Simultaneously Stage 3 is further away (VXN 29.03 vs the sub-25 requirement). The regime is pinned in Stage 4 by genuinely opposing evidence — not by indecision.
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 16, 2026 — the VXN re-spike above 27 + semis-led all-red tape (TSMC capex / Alphabet Gemini delay). **Unchanged; this read extends the same episode rather than starting a new one.**
- **Position:** Stage 4, ~3 sessions in, **deepening within the Nasdaq but not broadening into a systemic event.** **Stage-4 grind (60%, up from 50%):** VXN holds 27–30, VIX chops 16–20, NDX keeps underperforming without broad capitulation — this is now the modal path and the snapshot fits it precisely. **Stage-3 re-stabilization (25%, unchanged):** VXN falls back through 25 on a semis bounce; VIX's −4.95% is the first ingredient. **Stage-5 escalation (15%, down from 25%):** requires VIX to reverse back above 20 and credit to widen; VIX falling and Brent fading both argue against near-term escalation.
- **Confidence: Medium.** **Downgraded from Medium-High.** Why lower despite an unchanged stage: (1) **the two vol gauges now disagree**, and the disagreement is partly a one-session timing artifact I cannot resolve — a Jul-20 VXN print might materially change the read; (2) **no live TQQQ price this read** — the instrument that the entire Layer-2 analysis is about is unpriced, and the prior read's Jul-17 IBKR print (~$67.21) is now a session stale; (3) **HY OAS unmeasured Week 12** — the contained-vs-spreading discriminator, blind for a twelfth week, is *exactly* the gauge this read's central question needs; (4) breadth internals still unretrievable (Week 12). The stage call is well-supported; the *direction of travel* is not.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3):** **VXN closes below 25** AND **VIX holds below 16** AND **NDX posts a green day led by semis** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA (~$68).**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX reverses back above 20 and holds** OR **VXN closes above 30** OR **the drawdown broadens (SPX through −5% vs 52W high, DJI through −4%)** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 12).**

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds Stage 4 (regime-change) — Medium confidence, downgraded from Medium-High.** The defining development is a **sharp divergence between the two volatility gauges: VXN made a new episode high at 29.03 (+6.18%) while VIX fell to 17.84 (−4.95%)** — Nasdaq-specific fear accelerating as broad-market fear eases. Corroborating that on same-session data, **NDX extended to −7.1% vs its 52-week high and the Composite to −6.1%, while SPX and DJI both sat at just −2.1% — a 5-percentage-point dispersion, the widest of this sequence.** The prior read's central open question — whether the AI/semis derating stays contained or spreads — has resolved toward **contained but intensifying.** Against that, two genuine constructives: **VIX moved away from the Stage-5 threshold**, and **Brent's oil re-ignition failed (86.98, −1.27%)**, retracting the prior read's cross-asset downgrade. Total holds at **−4** with offsetting internal moves (Dealer −1 → −2, Cross-asset −1 → 0). **Confidence falls to Medium because the VIX/VXN divergence is partly a one-session timing artifact, no live TQQQ price was available this read, and HY OAS — precisely the gauge that would settle contained-vs-spreading — is unmeasured for a twelfth week.**

## Diverging Signals
- **THE GOVERNING TENSION — VXN at a new episode high while VIX falls.** These two gauges normally move together; they have separated. The straight reading is that risk is being repriced *specifically* in the Nasdaq complex, which is exactly where a 3×-NDX instrument lives. **The honest caveat: VXN is a Jul-17 close and VIX a Jul-20 intraday print, so part of the gap is a one-session timing offset, not a pure signal.** Both are stated because the divergence drives the read and the reader must be able to discount it.
- **The dispersion evidence is timing-independent, and that is why the Nasdaq conclusion survives the caveat.** NDX −7.1% vs SPX −2.1% and DJI −2.1% are all measured on the *same* Jul-17 close. A 5pp gap on same-session data confirms Nasdaq concentration without relying on the VIX/VXN comparison at all.
- **Contained vs spreading — resolving toward contained, but the definitive gauge is still blind.** SPX and DJI at −2.1% and a falling VIX say this is not systemic. But **HY OAS has been unmeasured for twelve weeks**, and credit is precisely what discriminates a contained equity derating from the front edge of something broader. The conclusion is directional, not settled.
- **Two constructives sit inside a bearish read and are not suppressed:** VIX −4.95% (moving away from Stage 5) and Brent −1.27% (oil-hedge thesis failed). Both were given effect — Stage-5 probability cut 25% → 15%, Cross-asset upgraded −1 → 0.
- **A prior-read call is retracted here.** Last read scored Cross-asset −1 on a re-igniting oil↑+gold↑ hedge complex; Brent has now reversed. **That was an over-read of a single-session oil move — the same error this sequence made on VXN in both directions.** Recorded explicitly rather than quietly re-scored.
- **The flat total (−4 → −4) is not stasis.** Two lenses moved a full point in opposite directions. A reader tracking only the headline number would miss that the vol complex deteriorated while the cross-asset complex improved.

## -- TQQQ INSTRUMENT ANALYSIS --

> ⚠️ **DATA-INTEGRITY NOTICE — READ BEFORE THE TABLE.** **No live TQQQ price was available for this read.** The supplied snapshot contains no TQQQ row, and per the hard rules I do not invent prices. The most recent authoritative print is the **prior read's IBKR live snapshot: ~$67.21 on Jul 17, 2026**, which is now **~1 session stale.** Critically, **NDX fell a further −1.49% on Jul 17**, which at 3× leverage implies TQQQ moved roughly **−4.5%** — so the true current price is plausibly nearer **~$64**, but **that is an inference, not a quote, and is not used as the analytical anchor.** Every TQQQ figure below is marked STALE or ESTIMATE. **The Layer-2 conclusions rest on VXN, NDX, and structural levels — which are real, current data — not on a precise TQQQ price.**

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** **Estimated oversold (RSI ~30–37) and still falling** — three-to-four consecutive down sessions with NDX leading losses each day. **Oversold-and-still-declining, not oversold-and-stabilizing.** No mean-reversion tailwind is evidenced. Marked estimate: no current RSI is retrievable and searchable values are pre-rout.
- **Market structure: DOWNTURN — confirmed on both legs, and more firmly than last read.** Leg 1: spot (even at the stale $67.21) is **−23.7% below the 4-wk high** of $88.09 — well past the −20% threshold; if the ~$64 inference holds it is ~−27%. Leg 2: **VXN 29.03 ≥ 25 — and now at an episode high**, not merely above the line. **Both legs satisfied with margin.** RALLY is not remotely in play (~+31% away).
- **Trend: below both moving averages.** ~12% under the 50DMA ($76.39) and below the 200DMA ($68.09), which broke on Jul 17. **The 200DMA has flipped from support to overhead resistance** at just +1.3% from the stale spot — the first level any stabilization attempt must reclaim.
- **Volatility & premium environment: the richest and most dangerous of this sequence.** VXN **29.03 at a new episode high**, est. TQQQ IV **~87%** (up from ~82%), EM **±12.3%** (up from ±11.6%). **Premium is expanding for the third consecutive session.** The single most important characterization: **this vol is being manufactured by the drawdown in the exact index the instrument is levered to** — VXN is rising while VIX falls. Premium richness and directional danger are the same phenomenon here, not two independent variables.
- **Put-selling context: HOSTILE — held, and marginally worse than last read.** The backdrop is hostile because: (1) **DOWNTURN is confirmed on both legs with margin** — −23.7%+ below the 4-wk high and VXN at an episode high; (2) **the decline has not stabilized** — three-to-four down sessions, NDX worst index two days running, drawdown extending ~1.5pp per session; (3) **the 200DMA and 4-wk low have flipped to overhead resistance**, so structure is now against any recovery; (4) **the ±12.3% one-week expected move is the widest of this sequence**, and on the inferred current level its lower bound sits essentially on the 13-wk low; (5) **HY OAS is unmeasured for a twelfth week**, so whether this derating stays contained is directionally-answered but not settled. **Two genuine mitigants, stated for balance:** VIX fell 4.95% (broad-market stress easing, Stage-5 risk reduced to 15%), and the oil-hedge complex failed to re-ignite. **Neither addresses the Nasdaq-specific problem** — and the Nasdaq is what this instrument is levered to. **Net: HOSTILE. The premium is the richest it has been in this sequence precisely because the underlying is in an accelerating, structurally-broken decline; those are not separable facts.** Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 / stabilization):** **VXN closes below 25** AND **VIX holds below 16** AND **NDX posts a semis-led green day** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA (~$68).** VIX's −4.95% is the first ingredient in place; the other three are not.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VXN closes above 30** (only ~1 point away) OR **VIX reverses back above 20 and holds** OR **the drawdown broadens — SPX through −5% vs 52W high or DJI through −4%** OR **TQQQ breaks the 13-wk low (~$57)** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 12).**
- **Neutralizer:** **the VIX/VXN divergence resolving.** If a fresh VXN print converges *down* toward the falling VIX, the read softens materially toward Stage 3. If VIX instead reverses *up* toward VXN, the derating is spreading and Stage 5 opens. **This single relationship is the highest-information thing to watch next session** — and a current VXN print would also resolve the timing-artifact caveat above. The **Jul 28–29 FOMC** and the **Jul 27–31 mega-cap earnings cluster** are the next scheduled pivots.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **~Jul 20–23, 2026:** **Netflix + first-wave tech earnings** — sentiment-sensitive into an active AI-valuation derating, but no QQQ mega-cap (Mag-7) report confirmed inside the next 5 sessions.
- **Jul 28–29, 2026:** **FOMC decision** (press conference Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET) — the next critical decision event, ~6 trading sessions out, **OUTSIDE** the current window.
- **Week of Jul 27–31, 2026:** **Mega-cap (Mag-7) earnings cluster** — the next major concentration-risk catalyst for NDX/TQQQ, landing directly into the AI-valuation debate now driving the tape.
- **Jul 30, 2026:** Q2 GDP Advance, 8:30 am ET.
- **Ongoing, unscheduled:** whether VXN converges to VIX or VIX reverses up to VXN; whether the AI/semis derating broadens beyond tech; whether the Brazil/60-country tariff threat is enacted further.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jul 20, 21, 22, 23, 24):** **none critical.** June CPI released Jul 14; **FOMC is Jul 28–29 — outside the window**; no Mag-7 earnings confirmed inside the window. Netflix/first-wave tech earnings begin ~Jul 20 but are not decision events.
- **Window: CLEAN-WINDOW** — no scheduled FOMC decision, CPI, NFP, or major central-bank decision inside the next 5 sessions. **Residual risk remains elevated but is headline/flow-driven, not calendar-driven:** an accelerating AI/semis derating, a VXN break above 30, a VIX reversal above 20, further tariff enactment, or an early tech-earnings miss.

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Last **Jun 19, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 18, 2026** — **~42 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Last **Jun 30, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 30, 2026** — **~50 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Net:** No structural calendar items inside the 2-session window. Intraday volume and pinning behavior are **not** structurally elevated by calendar mechanics. **The VXN new high, the second consecutive all-red Nasdaq-led tape, and the extended NDX drawdown are genuine directional signals — not expiry or rebalancing artifacts.** Descriptive only — no trade calls.
